desc: Restarts the local or a remote computer
reason: Causes immediate system restart, interrupting all running processes and unsaved work

desc: Shuts down the local or a remote computer
reason: Causes immediate shutdown with no recovery path from within the session

desc: Resets the computer to a previous state
reason: Can trigger system restore or factory reset, causing data loss

desc: CMD command to shut down or restart the system
reason: Directly shuts down or restarts Windows, bypassing any OS safety checks

desc: Opens the Windows Registry Editor GUI
reason: Provides unrestricted read/write access to the Windows Registry

desc: CMD tool for reading and writing the Windows Registry
reason: Can add, delete, import, or overwrite any registry key including critical system entries

desc: Sets the value of a property for an item, commonly used on registry paths
reason: Directly modifies registry values and file properties, can corrupt system configuration

desc: Deletes a property and its value from an item (e.g., registry key)
reason: Permanently removes registry properties, can break application or OS configuration

desc: Creates a new property for an item and sets its value
reason: Can create registry entries that alter OS or application behavior at startup or runtime

desc: Formats a volume with a specified file system
reason: Irreversibly erases all data on the target volume

desc: Cleans an entire disk by removing all partition information
reason: Wipes partition table from the disk, causing total data loss

desc: Initializes a disk for use by Windows
reason: Overwrites disk partition structure, destroying existing data

desc: CMD disk partitioning tool
reason: Can create, delete, or format partitions and volumes with no undo option

desc: CMD tool to edit the Boot Configuration Data store
reason: Modifying boot entries can render the system unbootable

desc: CMD tool to repair the master boot record and boot sector
reason: Incorrect use can destroy the boot record, making the system unbootable

desc: Changes the PowerShell script execution policy
reason: Disabling execution restrictions allows unsigned or malicious scripts to run

desc: Evaluates and runs a string as a PowerShell command
reason: Executes arbitrary code from a string, a common vector for injection attacks

desc: Alias for Invoke-Expression
reason: Same risk as Invoke-Expression; frequently used in obfuscated malicious scripts

desc: CMD utility covering user, group, service, and share management
reason: Can add/remove users, change passwords, manage groups, and control services

desc: Creates a new local user account
reason: Can be used to create unauthorized accounts for persistent access

desc: Deletes a local user account
reason: Permanently removes a user account and access, potentially locking out legitimate users

desc: Modifies a local user account (password, description, expiration)
reason: Can change passwords and disable accounts, causing loss of access

desc: Adds a member to a local security group
reason: Can grant administrator or other elevated privileges to any account

desc: Removes a member from a local security group
reason: Can revoke access for legitimate users from critical groups

desc: Permanently deletes a Windows service
reason: Irreversibly removes a service registration; can disable critical system services

desc: CMD Service Controller — creates, deletes, and configures Windows services
reason: Can delete, disable, or misconfigure any Windows service including system-critical ones

desc: Disables a network adapter
reason: Cuts network connectivity, potentially causing loss of remote access

desc: Modifies an existing Windows Firewall rule
reason: Altering firewall rules can expose the system to network attacks

desc: Creates a new Windows Firewall rule
reason: Can open ports or allow inbound traffic, weakening system security

desc: Deletes a Windows Firewall rule
reason: Removing firewall rules exposes the system to previously blocked traffic

desc: CMD network configuration tool for firewall, IP, and interface settings
reason: Can modify firewall policies, IP addresses, and proxy settings system-wide

desc: Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool
reason: Can add, remove, or corrupt Windows components and features

desc: Disables an optional Windows feature
reason: Can disable features that other applications or the OS depends on

desc: WMI Command-line interface for system configuration and administration
reason: Provides unrestricted access to hardware, processes, services, and system configuration

desc: System File Checker — scans and repairs protected system files
reason: Can overwrite or restore system files, potentially causing instability if misused

desc: Clears all entries from a Windows event log
reason: Destroys security and audit logs, eliminating forensic evidence of prior activity

desc: Runs commands on local or remote computers via PowerShell remoting
reason: Can execute arbitrary code on remote machines, enabling lateral movement

desc: Configures the computer to receive PowerShell remote commands
reason: Opens WinRM listener, exposing the system to remote command execution

desc: Disables PowerShell remoting on the local computer
reason: Can cut off legitimate remote management and monitoring access

desc: CMD tool to create, modify, delete, and run scheduled tasks
reason: Can establish persistent execution of arbitrary commands or scripts
